key_insight: MXtalGFlow turns molecular crystal generation into sampling a thermodynamic distribution, rather than producing isolated candidate structures.
engineering_relevance: This provides a principled way to explore polymorph landscapes and identify experimentally relevant crystal packings for materials design.
method: Defines a canonical crystal parameterization for molecules and space groups.
Trains energy-based GFlowNets to match a target Boltzmann distribution over crystal structures.
Analyzes sampled distributions around known polymorphs and searches for new competitive packing modes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on two molecules using two energy models (Lennard-Jones and the Universal Model for Atoms), with comparison to known polymorphs and newly identified packing modes.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen parameterization and energy function, so distribution quality may shift if the physical model or space-group assumptions are inaccurate.
paper_title: A Distributional Framework for Generative Modeling of Molecular Crystals
